mission.title: Install and Configure Velero (Backup & Restore Operator)
mission.description: Velero is a powerful tool for backing up and restoring Kubernetes cluster resources and persistent volumes. It is particularly useful for disaster recovery and migrating applications between clusters. Key trade-offs include the need for proper configuration of cloud storage providers and potential complexity in managing backups.

steps.title: Add the Velero Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Velero Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add vmware-tanzu https://vmware-tanzu.github.io/helm-charts

```

> This is the official repository for Velero.

steps.title: Install Velero CRDs
steps.description:
Before installing Velero, we need to install the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Velero to function.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/vmware-tanzu/velero/releases/download/v1.18.0/velero-crds.yaml

```

> This command installs the necessary CRDs for Velero.

steps.title: Install Velero
steps.description:
This step installs Velero using Helm, specifying the necessary configuration options.

```bash

helm install velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --create-namespace --version 1.18.0 --set configuration.provider=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.name=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.bucket=my-velero-bucket --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.prefix=velero --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.name=aws --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.region=us-west-2

```

> Replace 'my-velero-bucket' with your actual S3 bucket name.
commands: helm install velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --create-namespace --version 1.18.0 --set configuration.provider=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.name=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.bucket=my-velero-bucket --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.prefix=velero --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.name=aws --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.region=us-west-2

steps.title: Create a Backup
steps.description:
This step creates a backup of your Kubernetes resources using Velero.

```bash

velero backup create my-backup --include-namespaces '*' --wait

```

> This command creates a backup of all namespaces.

steps.title: Verify Velero Installation
steps.description:
This step checks if Velero is installed correctly and running.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n velero

```

> You should see the Velero server pod running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Velero
uninstall.description:
This step will remove Velero and all associated resources. Be cautious as this may lead to data loss if backups are not stored externally.

```bash

helm uninstall velero --namespace velero

kubectl delete namespace velero

```

> This command removes the Velero release.
> This command deletes the Velero namespace and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Velero
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your current configuration. This step will upgrade Velero to the latest version.

```bash

velero backup create pre-upgrade-backup --include-namespaces '*' --wait

helm upgrade velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --version 1.18.0

kubectl get pods -n velero

```

> Create a backup before upgrading.
> Upgrade Velero to the specified version.
> Verify that the Velero pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create backup: unable to get backup storage location'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates that Velero cannot access the specified backup storage location.

**Fix:**
Check your S3 bucket permissions and ensure the bucket exists.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found in velero namespace'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that Velero is not installed correctly or the namespace is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure Velero is installed in the correct namespace and check the installation logs.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create volume snapshot'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error may occur if the volume snapshot feature is not enabled in your Kubernetes cluster.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the volume snapshot controller is installed and running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Backup failed: unable to list resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the Velero service account does not have the necessary permissions.

**Fix:**
Check the RBAC permissions for the Velero service account and ensure it has access to the required resources.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.35, improved AWS S3 performance, and enhanced error messages.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.17 or earlier, ensure to migrate your backup storage location configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see the Velero server pod running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n velero'.
codeSnippets: helm install velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --create-namespace --version 1.18.0 --set configuration.provider=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.name=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.bucket=my-velero-bucket --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.prefix=velero --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.name=aws --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.region=us-west-2

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
